summary: French Academic painter of idealized figures. Known for The Birth of Venus, Nymphs and Satyr, The Young Shepherdess, The Bohemian, and nude women on seashores. Prominent alongside Alexandre Cabanel. Won Prix de Rome at 25. Wife Elizabeth Jane Gardner imitated his style.

works.name: The Birth of Venus (1879)
works.description: Title red-haired goddess stands on a conch shell, surrounded by cupids and mermen. A stream of cupids appears in the sky. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Title goddess stands on a conch/clam shell surrounded by cupids and mermen/centaurs; two mermen welcome her
examples: Two mermen welcome the title red-haired goddess, who stands on a conch shell, just like in the way-more famous painting by this name which is in the Uffizi
showed the title goddess in the middle of the ocean surrounded by a crowd of adoring nymphs and centaurs in his 1879 painting The Birth of Venus

works.name: Nymphs and Satyr
works.description: Four naked women dragging a faun/satyr into a pool.

clues.clue: Four naked women drag a faun/satyr into a pool
examples: another shows four naked women dragging a faun into a pool

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: French Academic painter. Contemporary of Alexandre Cabanel. Won Prix de Rome at 25 with Zenobia Found by Shepherds. Elizabeth Jane Gardner married him and imitated his style. Painted innocent-looking girls including The Young Shepherdess, The Bohemian, Alone in the World, The Knitting Girl. Associated with Academic art and l'art pompier.

clues.clue: Leading practitioner of Academic art, alongside Alexandre Cabanel
examples: Bouguereau and Cabanel were prominent members of this largely French artistic movement
Bouguereau was a leading practitioner of this style of art, which favored idealized, allegorical subjects

clues.clue: Academic art was derisively called 'l'art pompier' (fireman art) due to Attic helmets resembling firemen's headgear
examples: Critics savaged academism by labeling it as this kind of 'art.' The use of this French term derived from the fact that certain public safety officials in France wore headgear similar to the Attic helmets shown in academic art

clues.clue: Painted portraits of innocent young girls: The Young Shepherdess, The Bohemian, Alone in the World, The Knitting Girl
examples: did portraits of young gypsy girls with titles like The Young Shepherdess, The Bohemian, and Alone in the World
used the same young model in his paintings The Shepherdess and The Bohemian

clues.clue: Won Prix de Rome at 25 with Zenobia Found by Shepherds on the Banks of the Araxes
examples: won the Prix de Rome at the age of 25 with Zenobia Found by Shepherds on the Banks of the Araxes

clues.clue: Elizabeth Jane Gardner married him, stated she'd 'rather be known as the best imitator of [him] than be nobody'
examples: Elizabeth Jane Gardner married this painter and adopted his typical subjects, stating that she'd 'rather be known as the best imitator of [this man] than be nobody'

clues.clue: Bouguereau and Cabanel refused to allow Impressionists to exhibit in Paris Salon of 1863, leading to Salon des Refuses
examples: Bouguereau and Alexandre Cabanel refused to allow the Impressionists to exhibit their works in the Paris Salon of 1863, leading to the establishment of this alternative salon

clues.clue: First Mourning: Adam and Eve discovering the body of Abel, painted after death of his son
examples: This artist's painting of Adam and Eve discovering the body of Abel, First Mourning, was painted shortly after the death of his son

clues.clue: The Wave (1896): exemplary of his favored genre of nude women on the seashore
examples: whose 1896 work The Wave is exemplary of one of his favored genres, namely, nude women on the seashore

comprehensive_summary:
William-Adolphe Bouguereau was a French Academic painter of the 19th century, one of the leading practitioners of Academic art alongside his contemporary Alexandre Cabanel. He is most commonly identified by his idealized depictions of the human figure and genre scenes of innocent young girls.

His most notable works include The Birth of Venus (1879), in which the title red-haired goddess stands on a conch shell surrounded by cupids and mermen, and Nymphs and Satyr, depicting four women dragging a faun into a pool. He painted many portraits of young girls including The Young Shepherdess, The Bohemian, Alone in the World, and The Knitting Girl.

Bouguereau won the Prix de Rome at age 25 with Zenobia Found by Shepherds on the Banks of the Araxes. His wife, the American-born salon painter Elizabeth Jane Gardner, imitated his style, stating she'd 'rather be known as the best imitator of [him] than be nobody.' His painting First Mourning (Adam and Eve discovering Abel's body) was painted after the death of his own son.

Bouguereau and Cabanel refused to allow the Impressionists into the Paris Salon of 1863, leading to the Salon des Refuses. Academic art was derisively called 'l'art pompier' (fireman art) because Attic helmets in academic paintings resembled firemen's headgear. Other practitioners included Paul Delaroche and Jean-Leon Gerome.
